name: Cleveland State Bank
description_short: Community bank headquartered in Wisconsin offering personal banking, business banking, home lending, and investment services across three local locations.
description_long:
Cleveland State Bank is a Wisconsin-based community bank that positions itself as a locally-focused financial institution dedicated to nurturing the financial well-being of its community. The bank operates three physical locations, including branches in Elkhart Lake and Howards Grove, and emphasizes personal relationships and local decision-making as core to its business model. Founded on principles of accessibility and community engagement, the bank has maintained long-standing customer relationships, as evidenced by testimonials from clients with multi-year histories and multiple product usage.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of financial services across four main categories: personal banking (checking, savings, and personal accounts), business banking (cash management solutions and business accounts), home and lending (mortgages, home equity lines of credit, refinancing, bridge loans, and auto financing), and investment services through its Cleveland State Bank Investment Center (investment management, savings planning, and financial planning). Their website emphasizes convenience through both in-person and online banking capabilities.

Cleveland State Bank distinguishes itself through emphasis on personalized service, naming specific loan officers in customer testimonials and highlighting staff tenure (noting employees with 10 and 45 years of service). The bank markets itself as offering a banking experience "like banking back in the day" with helpful staff willing to accommodate customer needs. They provide transition support through a formal "5-Step Switch Kit" for customers switching from other financial institutions, and they host community events like document shred days.

As a regional community bank, Cleveland State Bank's primary limitation is geographic—service is restricted to Wisconsin locations. Customers outside the three branch areas would be unable to access in-person banking services. The website provides limited detail on specific product features, rates, fees, or competitive positioning relative to larger regional or national banks. No information is available regarding FDIC insurance limits, specific lending criteria, investment product details, or digital banking capabilities beyond mention of online account access.
services: Business banking and cash management solutions
pros: Three physical branch locations in Wisconsin allowing in-person service and relationship banking
Offers full-service lending including mortgages, home equity lines, bridge loans, and auto financing
Dedicated Investment Center for comprehensive financial planning and investment services
Demonstrated long customer tenure with multi-year relationships and complex product usage
Named loan officers with significant institutional tenure (10-45 year employees mentioned)
Formal customer transition support through 5-Step Switch Kit for account switching
Community engagement through events like document shred days and blog resources
cons: Limited geographic availability restricted to three Wisconsin locations with no online-only option mentioned
Website lacks specific product details including interest rates, fees, minimum balances, and lending terms
No information provided on investment product types, performance, or fee structures
Limited transparency on digital banking capabilities beyond mention of online access
No mention of mobile app, mobile banking, or technology-forward features relative to modern banking competitors
best_for: Wisconsin residents in Elkhart Lake, Howards Grove, or nearby areas seeking relationship-based community banking
Customers needing comprehensive lending services including mortgages, home equity, and business financing with local decision-makers
Families and small business owners valuing personal service and staff familiarity over digital-only banking
Customers transitioning from larger banks seeking a more personalized banking experience
diagnosis: Cleveland State Bank is best for Wisconsin residents within its three-location service area who prioritize personalized relationship banking and comprehensive lending services over digital convenience and competitive rate shopping. The main caveat is geographic limitation—this bank only serves customers who can visit physical branches in Wisconsin, making it unsuitable for customers outside those areas or those requiring 24/7 digital-first banking.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
